claim: Games are an effective benchmark for LLM capabilities because they are evolutionary: as models improve, the challenge depth increases automatically, avoiding saturation of the benchmark.
actionable: When designing LLM benchmarks, prioritize tasks with emergent complexity that scale with model capability; game-based evals offer self-scaling properties without manual recalibration.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: For Diplomacy specifically, context management is critical: representing only relevant units, adjacency, relationships over time (diaries), and consolidating past phases reduces token waste and improves model decision-making.
actionable: When building game harnesses for LLM evaluation, be opinionated about context: show only decision-relevant info, maintain relationship history, highlight unread messages, and consolidate past context to preserve tokens.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: Claude tends not to betray allies or deceive other players in Diplomacy, even when strategically advantageous; O3 is more willing to propose and execute deception, suggesting alignment training affects game strategy.
actionable: If you are designing adversarial evals or testing robustness, be aware that different models have different comfort levels with deception and rule-breaking; test both honest and strategic behaviors.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: Benchmarks spread as ideas and get adopted by the community; individual creators (like Simon Wilson's pelican benchmark) can drive model training and evaluation updates at scale.
actionable: Publish interesting, simple evals as blog posts or social media; if they resonate, they can influence how models are evaluated and trained; focus on things you personally care about.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: A well-designed harness can outperform the raw model; the multi-model harness with tools, context, and multi-step reasoning can achieve higher real-world performance than any single model alone.
actionable: When evaluating LLM-based systems, always test the full harness (model + tools + prompt engineering), not just the base model; harness design can be as important as model choice.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: You can use game trace data to create reasoning evals: record all LLM responses, decision logs, and reasoning; then use classifiers or rule-based checks to evaluate truthfulness and strategic quality.
actionable: Build game harnesses with comprehensive logging and replay capabilities; export trace data as JSON/CSV so you can analyze reasoning patterns and build custom evals post-hoc.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: Benchmarks are not just evaluation tools; they are communication tools that help non-AI people (yoga teachers, local business owners) understand and trust what AI can do for them.
actionable: When designing evals for a broader audience, make them tangible and relatable; let people see how the model performs on tasks they care about; use simple examples over complex metrics.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: Different models have observable personality differences in games: Deep Seek is flowery and roleplay-heavy; O3 is strategic and willing to lie; Claude is cooperative and nice.
actionable: Run the same benchmark across multiple models and compare not just scores but behavioral patterns; look for personality-driven differences that might affect real-world use cases.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: Building evals that work across model families requires harness flexibility and model-agnostic prompt engineering; test with cheaper models (e.g., 2.5 Flash) early and often to validate the harness.
actionable: Iteratively test harnesses with cheaper, faster models first; once the harness works at scale with cheap models, validate with frontier models; this saves on compute costs and catches harness issues early.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet

claim: Creative writing and complex human-centric tasks still lack standardized benchmarks; single-person benchmark creation is feasible but broader ecosystem adoption depends on community engagement and resonance.
actionable: For soft skills like creative writing, design benchmarks that combine quantitative metrics (e.g., style consistency) with qualitative evaluation; publish and iterate based on community feedback.
source_title: Launching AI Diplomacy: the hardest LLM Game Benchmark yet
